rigor.trigger: material alternatives differ on measurable properties
rigor.tier_reason: the decision is reversible but bears downstream load

decision_frame.question: Keep the status quo or adopt option A?
decision_frame.system_boundary: The versioned example component and its direct consumers.

coverage.reason: No functional behavior changes inside the boundary.
No data representation changes inside the boundary.
No concurrent history changes inside the boundary.
No replicated state exists inside the boundary.
Recovery time is an authorized objective.
The trust boundary and authorities do not change.
Capacity and resource bounds are outside the question.
No probabilistic or numerical claim bears load.
The synthesis applies an operator-authorized constraint rule.

derivations.construct: bounded recovery under the declared single-fault model
derivations.model: One component failure at a time; rollback artifact remains reachable.

preconditions.statement: The rollback artifact is revision-pinned and reachable.

steps: The declared failure preserves the rollback artifact.
Invoking rollback restores the prior contract within the stated horizon.

result.statement: Option A satisfies the bounded recovery constraint in the declared model.

residual_mismatch: Correlated artifact-store failure is outside the model.

basis: A satisfies the hard contract and the authorized recovery objective in the declared model.
concessions: Correlated artifact-store failure remains outside scope.
recovery_moves: Retain the prior revision until the validity window closes.
